Title: HY5: a key regulator for light-mediated nutrient uptake and utilization by plants

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: carbon assimilates; nitrogen; sulfur-containing metabolites; phosphate; copper; iron
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: carbohydrate; mineral nutrient; amino acid-related sulfur metabolites
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports plant growth, photosynthesis, respiration, chlorophyll biosynthesis, ATP production, and nutrient homeostasis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a master positive regulator linking light signaling to uptake, transport, and assimilation of multiple nutrients; can activate or repress nutrient-related target genes depending on pathway
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HY5 is a bZIP transcription factor and master regulator of light-mediated nutrient uptake and utilization. 2. HY5 directly regulates genes involved in carbon assimilation and transport, nitrogen uptake and assimilation, sulfur uptake and assimilation, phosphate acquisition, copper homeostasis, and iron uptake/homeostasis. 3. HY5 can move from shoot to root and activate root nutrient programs. 4. HY5 function in nutrient regulation is described as conserved across plant species including Arabidopsis, tomato, cotton, apple, and rice-related contexts.
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus; mobile between shoot and root tissues
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Light-responsive; accumulates in shoots and can induce its own expression in roots
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Light-activated in shoots; active under nutrient deficiency contexts including low N, low P, Fe deficiency, Cu deficiency
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Light quality/signaling; low nitrogen; phosphate deficiency; iron deficiency; copper deficiency
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review article summarizing prior evidence including promoter binding, direct transcriptional activation/repression, mutant analysis, shoot-to-root mobility assays, and expression analysis
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Gene coexpression analysis under 13 nutrient stress conditions (reported from cited work); accession NA
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulation may alter root system architecture, nutrient balance, and broader light-regulated development; specific tradeoffs not established in this review
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for crop improvement to enhance multi-nutrient uptake and utilization under variable light and nutrient stress; review suggests cell type-specific genome editing or expression manipulation may improve growth and productivity, with caution due to HY5 pleiotropy
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is a review paper; HY5 is the primary summarized core gene. The paper also discusses orthologs including GhHY5, SlHY5/SIHY5, MbHY5, and MdHY5 as conserved functional counterparts in different species.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports plant growth and nitrogen nutrition
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes root nitrogen uptake by inducing GhNRT1.1 after light activation in shoot and transport to root
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhHY5 is induced by light in shoots. 2. GhHY5 is transported to the root through the phloem. 3. Root GhHY5 activates its own expression and induces GhNRT1.1 expression, promoting nitrogen uptake.
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus; mobile through phloem from shoot to root
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by light in shoots; active in roots after transport
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review-based summary of prior light induction, phloem transport, root expression activation, and nitrogen uptake analyses
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to improve nitrogen uptake efficiency in cotton under light-responsive management
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Orthologous HY5 function supports conservation of light-nutrient signaling.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports phosphorus uptake and iron uptake/homeostasis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activated by red light/phyB in shoots, then promotes arbuscular mycorrhizal symbiosis-dependent P uptake and induces FER-dependent Fe uptake in roots
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlHY5 is activated by red light perceived by phyB in shoots. 2. SlHY5 promotes arbuscular mycorrhizal symbiosis development and phosphorus uptake under Pi deficiency. 3. SlHY5 moves or signals from shoot to root and induces root Fe uptake by activating FER expression.
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus; mobile between shoot and root
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Activated in shoots by red light; induced in roots downstream of shoot-derived signaling
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Same as source species; active under Pi deficiency and Fe-related conditions
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of prior red-light signaling, mobility/expression induction, and nutrient uptake assays
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target for improving phosphorus acquisition and iron uptake in tomato, especially under low-P conditions
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper uses SIHY5 spelling in places; interpreted as SlHY5 (tomato HY5 ortholog).

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports nitrate assimilation and plant nutrition
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates nitrate assimilation through positive regulation of MdNIA2 and MdNRT1.1
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdHY5 positively regulates nitrate assimilation in apple. 2. It positively regulates MdNIA2 and MdNRT1.1 expression.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of prior gene expression and nitrate assimilation analyses
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for improving nitrogen use efficiency in apple
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as conserved HY5 function in nitrate assimilation.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports iron transport and alleviates Fe deficiency chlorosis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates MbYSL7 under Fe deficiency, promoting Fe transport and alleviating chlorosis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MbHY5 positively regulates MbYSL7 expression under Fe deficiency. 2. This regulation alleviates Fe deficiency-induced chlorosis and promotes Fe transport.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of prior expression and Fe-deficiency phenotype analyses
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for breeding Fe-efficient apple rootstocks or cultivars with reduced chlorosis
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Important for Fe transport rather than direct Fe uptake enzyme control.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: carbon assimilates; nitrogen; sulfur-containing metabolites; phosphate; copper; iron
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: carbohydrate; mineral nutrient; amino acid-related sulfur metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Shoots and roots; chloroplast-related Cu context; root tissues for uptake processes
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports plant growth, photosynthesis, respiration, chlorophyll biosynthesis, ATP production, and nutrient homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Central light-responsive regulator that activates or represses nutrient uptake, transport, and assimilation genes across multiple nutrient pathways
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master regulator linking light perception to multi-nutrient uptake and utilization
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Light activated/accumulated in shoots; induced under nutrient deficiency contexts; root expression can be activated by mobile shoot-derived HY5
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts together with SPL7 in copper homeostasis network
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Pleiotropic developmental effects likely because HY5 regulates c. 3000 genes; specific tradeoffs not detailed in review
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Gene coexpression analysis under 13 nutrient stress conditions from cited study; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: TPS1; SWEET11; SWEET12; NIA2; NIR1; NRT1.1/NRT1.2; AMT1;2/AMT1.2; NRT2.1; SULTR1;2; APR1; APR2; PHT1;1; LPR1; MIR408; FRO2; FIT; IRT1; BTS; PYE
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Carbon assimilation and transport; nitrogen uptake and assimilation; sulfur uptake and assimilation; phosphate acquisition; copper homeostasis; iron uptake and homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Light; red light; blue light; low nitrogen; phosphate deficiency; copper deficiency; iron deficiency
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review article, not a primary experimental paper. HY5 is the sole primary summarized core regulator of the paper; target genes are supporting/background genes from cited studies rather than coequal core genes here.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering broad nutrient efficiency, including cell type-specific editing/manipulation under combined nutrient deficiencies and different light environments
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Photoreceptors including phyB, CRY1, CRY2; nutrient deficiency conditions; Cu status via SPL7 network
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates many nutrient uptake/assimilation genes including TPS1, SWEET11/12, NIA2, NIR1, NRT2.1, SULTR1;2, APR1/2, PHT1;1, LPR1, MIR408, FRO2, FIT, IRT1; represses BTS, PYE, and some uptake genes such as NRT1.1/NRT1.2 and AMT1.2 depending on context
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Can activate its own expression in roots after shoot-to-root movement in some species; broader feedback not established
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review synthesis of promoter binding, mutant phenotyping, gene expression assays, mobility studies, and nutrient uptake/assimilation measurements
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HY5 is a master regulator of light-mediated nutrient uptake and utilization. 2. HY5 directly binds promoters of many nutrient-related genes and can act as activator or repressor. 3. HY5 functions in shoot-to-root signaling to coordinate nutrient acquisition with shoot carbon status. 4. HY5-mediated nutrient regulation is conserved across several plant species.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Light-responsive; accumulates in shoots; root expression induced by mobile HY5 in some systems
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus; mobile signal between shoot and root

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports nitrogen nutrition and plant growth
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes root nitrogen uptake by inducing GhNRT1.1 after light-induced shoot activation and phloem transport
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Conserved mobile HY5 regulator controlling nitrogen uptake in cotton
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by light in shoots; transported to roots; activates root expression
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GhNRT1.1; self-activation of GhHY5 in root
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the review highlights conserved HY5 ortholog function across species.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulator for improving nitrogen uptake efficiency in cotton
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates GhNRT1.1 and root GhHY5 expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of prior mobility, expression, and nitrogen uptake studies
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhHY5 is light induced in shoots. 2. It travels through phloem to root. 3. It activates root GhHY5 and GhNRT1.1 to enhance nitrogen uptake.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Light-induced in shoot and active in root after transport

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports phosphorus acquisition and iron uptake
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes P uptake under Pi deficiency and activates FER-mediated Fe uptake downstream of red light/phyB
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Conserved light-responsive HY5 regulator integrating red light with phosphorus and iron acquisition
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Activated in shoots by red light perceived by phyB; root expression induced downstream
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: FER; genes/processes promoting arbuscular mycorrhizal symbioses development
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Spelled SIHY5 in text due to font/OCR variation; treated as SlHY5.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for improving low-P adaptation and Fe nutrition in tomato
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates FER and promotes AMS-associated phosphorus uptake programs
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Induces root HY5 expression after shoot activation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of prior red-light signaling and nutrient uptake studies
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlHY5 acts downstream of phyB/red light. 2. It promotes AMS development and P uptake under Pi deficiency. 3. It activates FER to induce Fe uptake responses in tomato roots.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Shoot light induced; root induced downstream
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus; mobile between shoot and root

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates nitrate assimilation by activating MdNIA2 and MdNRT1.1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Conserved HY5 ortholog controlling nitrate assimilation in apple
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed in conservation section of review.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for enhancing nitrate use efficiency in apple
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Light likely, but not explicitly detailed here
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of prior gene regulation and nitrate assimilation evidence
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdHY5 positively regulates nitrate assimilation. 2. It activates MdNIA2 and MdNRT1.1.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports iron transport and relieves Fe deficiency chlorosis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates MbYSL7 under Fe deficiency to promote Fe transport
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Conserved HY5 ortholog regulating Fe transport under deficiency in apple
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Associated with chlorosis alleviation rather than direct uptake enzyme induction.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for improving Fe transport and reducing Fe deficiency chlorosis in apple
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of prior expression and Fe-deficiency phenotype evidence
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MbHY5 positively regulates MbYSL7 under Fe deficiency. 2. This promotes Fe transport and alleviates chlorosis.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports photosynthesis, respiration, and ATP production through copper homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts with HY5 to transcriptionally regulate MIR408 and thereby influence Cu allocation to chloroplast and plastocyanin levels under Cu deficiency
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Cu-responsive transcription factor central to HY5-SPL7 crosstalk controlling Cu homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: MIR408; several Cu-responsive miRNAs (as stated generally)
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cu deficiency; changing light and Cu conditions
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although the review centers on HY5, SPL7 is also specifically summarized as the Cu-responsive regulator partnering with HY5.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential co-target with HY5 to improve Cu use efficiency and photosynthetic performance under Cu limitation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates MIR408 and other Cu-responsive miRNAs
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Review summary of prior transcriptional regulation studies
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SPL7 is a Cu-responsive transcription factor. 2. SPL7 acts with HY5 to regulate MIR408. 3. This affects Cu transport to chloroplast and plastocyanin levels.
